---
title: "Meeting_06_02_2026"
output: github_document
---

Found that education is positively correlated with conscientiousness (as per [working document](Write_up_documents/Write_up_06_02_2026.pdf))

```{r echo=FALSE}
# ============================================================================
# WHEN YOU WOULD USE HECKMAN'S APPROACH WITH YOUR DATA
# ============================================================================

# Scenario: You have conscientiousness BUT you're worried about:
# 1. Measurement error in your conscientiousness scale
# 2. Multiple imperfect measures you want to combine
# 3. Reverse causality (education affects measured conscientiousness)
# 4. Selection bias

# Example: You have MULTIPLE non-cognitive measures
# your_data <- data.frame(
#   wage = ...,
#   education = ...,
#   
#   # Multiple non-cognitive measures (all imperfect proxies)
#   conscientiousness = ...,
#   grit_score = ...,
#   self_control = ...,
#   locus_of_control = ...,
#   
#   # Cognitive measures
#   iq_test = ...,
#   math_score = ...
# )

# ============================================================================
# STEP 1: Specify Measurement System
# ============================================================================

# Assume there's a latent "non-cognitive ability" that generates all these measures
# Similar to how IQ tests measure latent "g"

# Measurement equations:
# conscientiousness = β0 + α_conscientiousness * f_N + education_effects + error
# grit = β1 + α_grit * f_N + education_effects + error
# self_control = β2 + α_self_control * f_N + education_effects + error

# You need a structural equation modeling package
# library(lavaan)
# 
# # Define the latent factor model
# model_spec <- '
#   # Latent factors
#   NonCog =~ conscientiousness + grit_score + self_control + locus_of_control
#   Cog =~ iq_test + math_score
#   
#   # Allow education to affect measurements (key Heckman insight!)
#   conscientiousness ~ education
#   grit_score ~ education
#   self_control ~ education
#   
#   # Structural model: wage depends on latent factors
#   log_wage ~ NonCog + Cog + education + age + experience
# '
# 
# # Estimate (this is simplified - Heckman used Bayesian MCMC)
# fit <- sem(model_spec, data = your_data)
# summary(fit)
# 
# # Extract latent factor scores for each person
# factor_scores <- lavPredict(fit)
# your_data$f_noncog <- factor_scores[, "NonCog"]
# your_data$f_cog <- factor_scores[, "Cog"]
```


# Steps:

1. Specify latent factor model

" NonCog =~ conscientiousness"

Specify structural model: wage depends on latent factor with other observables

"log_wage ~ NonCog + education + age + etc."

2. Estimate structural model (Heckman used Bayesian MCMC)

3. Extract factor scores

4. Do nonparametric estimation with factor score obtained for conscientiousness from simulations
